text.title: A/B Testing Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding A/B Testing. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Hypothesis, primary metric, guardrail metrics, and minimum detectable effect are pre-registered before launch; post-hoc adjustment invalidates the experiment.

rationale.source: Kohavi, Tang, Xu — Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments

rule.rationale: Pre-registration is the only defence against peeking and goal-post moves.

rule.statement: Sample size is calculated from baseline conversion, MDE, 80 percent power, and 95 percent significance before launch and is not reduced once running.
rule.rationale: Stopping early on a positive trend inflates false positives.

rule.statement: Experiments run for at least one full business cycle (minimum two weeks) to capture weekday-weekend variation.
rule.rationale: Shorter windows produce biased samples that misrepresent typical behaviour.

rule.statement: Guardrail metrics (revenue, error rate, retention) are checked alongside the primary metric; a winner on the primary metric that violates a guardrail does not ship.
rule.rationale: Local wins that hurt overall metrics are net-negative.

rule.statement: Segment analyses are pre-specified; post-hoc segment slicing requires multiple-comparison correction.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc fishing finds false-positive segments in any dataset.

rule.statement: Statistically significant results must also clear a practical-significance threshold (e.g., +0.5 percent absolute) before shipping.
rule.rationale: Tiny statistically-significant effects rarely justify implementation cost.

rule.statement: Agent recommendations to ship are advisory; a named human (PM or analyst) signs off after reviewing context and risk.
rule.rationale: Agents cannot weigh organisation-specific risk tolerance.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ab-testing.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
